explanation: TRUE POSITIVE. The _recalculatePoolReward function (line 127) always advances lastRewardTime but only updates accRewardPerShareScaled when totalShares != 0. Reward tokens accruing during any zero-staker period (gap before first staker, periods between all unstaking, or no stakers ever) are silently skipped — neither distributed to users nor recoverable by the owner. The stopPool function only recovers future rewards ((oldEndTime - block.timestamp) * rewardPerSecondScaled / PRECISION) and cannot address past skipped periods; furthermore, Solidity 0.8 overflow protection causes stopPool to revert with an underflow if called after the pool naturally expires (block.timestamp > oldEndTime). No other owner-accessible recovery function exists in the contract. This results in permanent, unrecoverable loss of deposited reward tokens under realistic, commonly occurring conditions.
